evals.prompt: I just created documentation for the multiarch-tuning-operator repository at /Users/kpais/kpais-workspace/claude-tmp/multiarch-tuning-operator-test-plugin. I ran /agentic-docs:generate-evals and it created a promptfooconfig.yaml file with 43 test cases. Now I want to run the evaluation to see if the documentation is good. Can you evaluate it?
evals.expected_output: Should spawn code sub-agent to run promptfoo, collect metrics, spawn judge sub-agent to analyze results, and produce comprehensive evaluation report

assertions.name: detected_invalid_provider_config
assertions.description: v6.1 should detect the invalid Vertex AI provider format in promptfooconfig.yaml

assertions.name: provided_fix_instructions
assertions.description: Should provide clear instructions on how to fix the provider configuration

assertions.name: referenced_generate_evals_skill
assertions.description: Should reference the generate-evals skill documentation for the correct format

assertions.name: did_not_run_promptfoo
assertions.description: Should NOT run promptfoo when invalid config is detected

assertions.name: clear_next_steps
assertions.description: Should provide clear next steps (edit config or regenerate)

assertions.name: v60_runs_without_validation
assertions.description: v6.0 (baseline) should attempt to run promptfoo and encounter API errors

evals.prompt: I want to evaluate the agentic documentation in /tmp/test-repo but I haven't generated the evaluation config yet. What should I do?
evals.expected_output: Should detect missing promptfooconfig.yaml and provide helpful error message instructing user to run /agentic-docs:generate-evals first

assertions.name: detected_missing_config
assertions.description: Should detect that promptfooconfig.yaml is missing

assertions.name: showed_error_message
assertions.description: Should display the specific error message from the skill

assertions.name: did_not_spawn_subagents
assertions.description: Should NOT spawn code/judge sub-agents when config is missing

assertions.name: clear_next_steps
assertions.description: Should provide clear next steps for user

evals.prompt: okay so i just finished writing all the AGENTS.md and ai-docs/ stuff for our kubernetes operator. i want to make sure AI agents can actually find and use this documentation properly. i generated some test config earlier (i think it's called promptfooconfig.yaml or something?) and now i need to run those tests and see what passes/fails. also can you tell me which docs files are getting accessed the most? the repository is at ~/code/my-operator
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this as evaluation request, run promptfoo tests, collect session metrics showing file access patterns, and report both test results and documentation usage analytics
evals.setup_required: Repository with promptfooconfig.yaml, metrics plugin available

assertions.name: recognized_evaluation_request
assertions.description: Should recognize this casual request as an evaluation task

assertions.name: handled_uncertain_config_name
assertions.description: Should handle user's uncertainty about config filename

assertions.name: ran_promptfoo_tests
assertions.description: Should run promptfoo tests to answer 'see what passes/fails'

assertions.name: collected_file_access_metrics
assertions.description: Should collect and report file access metrics

assertions.name: answered_both_questions
assertions.description: Should address both test results AND file access metrics in response
